 {"id":"bd-2d5r","title":"Fix silent error handling in RPC response writing","description":"Marshal and write errors silently ignored in writeResponse, can send partial JSON and hang clients.

Location: internal/rpc/server_lifecycle_conn.go:228-232

Problem:
- json.Marshal error ignored - cyclic reference sends corrupt JSON
- Write error ignored - connection closed, no indication to caller  
- WriteByte error ignored - client hangs waiting for newline
- Flush error ignored - partial data buffered

Current code:
func (s *Server) writeResponse(writer *bufio.Writer, resp Response) {
    data, _ := json.Marshal(resp)  // Ignored!
    _, _ = writer.Write(data)      // Ignored!
    _ = writer.WriteByte('\n')     // Ignored!
    _ = writer.Flush()             // Ignored!
}

Solution: Return errors, handle in caller, close connection on error

Impact: Client hangs waiting for response; corrupt JSON sent

Effort: 1 hour","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-16T14:51:47.002242-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-16T15:04:00.481507-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-16T15:04:00.481507-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-2vh3.4","title":"Tier 3: AI-powered squash summarization","description":"## Design: Agent-Provided Summarization (Inversion of Control)

bd is a tool FOR agents, not an agent itself. The calling agent provides
the summary; bd just stores it.

### API

```bash
# Agent generates summary, passes to bd
bd mol squash bd-xxx --summary "Agent-generated summary here"

# Without --summary, falls back to basic concatenation
bd mol squash bd-xxx
```

### Gas Town Integration Pattern

```go
// In polecat completion handler or witness
raw := exec.Command("bd", "mol", "show", molID, "--json").Output()
summary := callHaiku(buildSummaryPrompt(raw))  // agent's job
exec.Command("bd", "mol", "squash", molID, "--summary", summary).Run()
```

### Why This Design

| Concern | bd's job | Agent's job |
|---------|----------|-------------|
| Store data | ✅ | |
| Query data | ✅ | |
| Generate summaries | | ✅ |
| Call LLMs | | ✅ |
| Manage API keys | | ✅ |

### Implementation Status

- [x] --summary flag added to bd mol squash
- [x] Tests for agent-provided summary
- [ ] Gas Town integration (separate task)

### Acceptance Criteria

- ✅ bd mol squash --summary uses provided text
- ✅ Without --summary, falls back to concatenation
- ✅ No LLM calls in bd itself","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:00.732749-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:29:16.288713-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:29:16.288713-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:00.733264-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:22.698686-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3.5","title":"Tier 4: Auto-squash on molecule completion","description":"Automatically squash molecules when they reach terminal state.

## Integration Points

1. Hook into molecule completion handler
2. Detect when all steps are done/failed
3. Trigger squash automatically

## Config

bd config set mol.auto_squash true  # Default: false
bd config set mol.auto_squash_on_success true  # Only on success
bd config set mol.auto_squash_delay '5m'  # Wait before squash

## Implementation Options

### Option A: Post-Completion Hook
In mol completion handler:
- Check if auto_squash enabled
- Call Squash() after terminal state

### Option B: Git Hook
In .beads/hooks/post-commit:
- bd mol squash --auto

### Option C: Daemon Background Task
- Daemon periodically checks for squashable molecules
- Squashes in background

## Acceptance Criteria

- Completed molecules auto-squash without manual intervention
- Configurable delay before squash
- Option to squash only on success vs always
- Works with both daemon and no-daemon modes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:13.345577-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:40:39.794527-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:40:39.794527-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:13.346152-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:22.797141-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3.6","title":"Tier 5 (Future): JSONL archive rotation","description":"Periodic rotation of issues.jsonl for long-running repos.

## Design

.beads/
├── issues.jsonl           # Current (hot)
├── archive/
│   ├── issues-2025-12.jsonl.gz  # Archived (cold)
│   └── ...
└── index.jsonl            # Merged index for queries

## Commands

bd archive rotate [flags]
  --older-than N    Archive issues closed > N days
  --compress        Gzip archives
  --dry-run         Preview

bd archive list           # Show archived periods
bd archive restore <period>  # Restore from archive

## Config

bd config set archive.enabled true
bd config set archive.rotate_days 90
bd config set archive.compress true
bd config set archive.path '.beads/archive'

## Considerations

- Archives can be gitignored (local only) or committed (shared)
- Query layer must check index, hydrate from archive
- Cold storage tiering (S3/GCS) for enterprise
- Merkle proofs preserved for audit

## Priority

This is post-1.0 work. Current focus is on squash (removes ephemeral).
Archive helps with long-term history but is less critical.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210008-08:00","updated_at":"2026-01-02T00:17:14.308269-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210543-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3","title":"Investigate and upgrade to modernc.org/sqlite 1.39.1+","description":"We had to pin modernc.org/sqlite to v1.38.2 due to a FOREIGN KEY constraint regression in v1.39.1 (SQLite 3.50.4).

**Issue:** [deleted:bd-47], GH #144

**Symptom:** CloseIssue fails with "FOREIGN KEY constraint failed (787)" when called via MCP/daemon, but works fine via CLI.

**Root Cause:** Unknown - likely stricter FK enforcement in SQLite 3.50.4 or modernc.org wrapper changes.

**Workaround:** Pinned to v1.38.2 (SQLite 3.49.x)

**TODO:**
1. Monitor modernc.org/sqlite releases for fixes
2. Check SQLite 3.50.5+ changelogs for FK-related fixes
3. Investigate why daemon mode fails but CLI succeeds (connection reuse? transaction isolation?)
4. Consider filing upstream issue with reproducible test case
5. Upgrade when safe","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-07T09:43:47.856354-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-07T15:06:26.240131-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-07T15:06:26.240131-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-5hrq","title":"bd doctor: detect issues referenced in commits but still open","description":"Add a doctor check that finds 'orphaned' issues - ones referenced in git commit messages (e.g., 'fix bug (bd-xxx)') but still marked as open in beads.

**Detection logic:**
1. Get all open issue IDs from beads
2. Parse git log for issue ID references matching pattern \(prefix-[a-z0-9.]+\)
3. Report issues that appear in commits but are still open

**Output:**
⚠ Warning: N issues referenced in commits but still open
  bd-xxx: 'Issue title' (commit abc123)
  bd-yyy: 'Issue title' (commit def456)
  
  These may be implemented but not closed. Run 'bd show <id>' to check.

**Implementation:**
- Add check to doctor/checks.go
- Use git log parsing (already have git utilities)
- Match against configured issue_prefix","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:48:08.473165-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:55:37.795109-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:55:37.795109-08:00"}

File: lib/test_beads_mail_adapter.py","notes":"Test suite completed with 29 comprehensive tests covering:
- Enabled mode (server available): 10 tests
- Disabled mode (server unavailable): 2 tests  
- Graceful degradation: 4 tests
- Reservation conflicts: 2 tests
- Configuration: 5 tests
- Health check scenarios: 3 tests
- HTTP error handling: 3 tests

**Performance**: All tests run in 10ms (fast!)

**Coverage highlights**:
✅ Server health checks (ok, degraded, error, timeout)
✅ All API operations (reserve, release, notify, check_inbox, get_reservations)
✅ HTTP errors (404, 409 conflict, 500, 503)
✅ Network errors (timeout, connection refused)
✅ Malformed responses (bad JSON, empty body, plain text errors)
✅ Environment variable configuration
✅ Graceful degradation when server dies mid-operation
✅ Conflict handling with both JSON and plain text errors
✅ Dict wrapper responses ({"messages": [...]} and {"reservations": [...]})
✅ Custom TTL for reservations
✅ Default agent name fallback

No external dependencies, no slow integration tests, just fast unit tests with mocks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-07T22:43:21.294596-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-08T01:32:39.906342-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-08T01:32:39.906342-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5ki8","depends_on_id":"bd-m9th","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-07T22:43:21.296024-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5l59","title":"Code smell: Issue struct is a God Object (50+ fields)","description":"attached_args: Refactor Issue struct God Object

The Issue struct in internal/types/types.go has 50+ fields covering many different concerns:

- Basic issue tracking (ID, Title, Description, Status, Priority)
- Messaging/communication (Sender, Ephemeral, etc.)
- Agent identity (HookBead, RoleBead, AgentState, RoleType, Rig)
- Gate/async coordination (AwaitType, AwaitID, Timeout, Waiters)
- Source tracing (SourceFormula, SourceLocation)
- HOP validation/entities (Creator, Validations)
- Compaction (CompactionLevel, CompactedAt, OriginalSize)
- Tombstones (DeletedAt, DeletedBy, DeleteReason)

Consider:
1. Extracting field groups into embedded structs (e.g., AgentFields, GateFields)
2. Using composition pattern to make the struct more modular
3. At minimum, grouping related fields together with section comments

Location: internal/types/types.go:12-82","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-28T15:31:34.021236-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-28T16:41:07.721864-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T16:41:07.721864-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5l59","depends_on_id":"bd-784c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-28T15:38:04.187103-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
